focus: release-readiness synthesis risk: confirm the complete fix set closes cleanly, no surface left half-fixed

findingsNew.title: Active graph metadata will not safely self-resolve from checkbox-only completion
findingsNew.claim: The prior expectation that graph-metadata.json will self-resolve after metadata regeneration is incomplete unless source wording is also corrected or generated metadata is explicitly patched after regeneration.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked whole-repo #spec-kit-documentation references and found no additional live anchor consumer outside README/spec/review evidence; checked current tasks source and found the retired label still present in active task text.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The generator might ignore completed task rows or quoted old labels, but no reviewed evidence proves that behavior and current metadata already demonstrates the retired label was derived into the entity catalog.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if metadata regeneration is proven to ignore completed task rows or quoted old labels, or if remediation explicitly updates tasks.md:72 wording before regenerating metadata.

findingDetails.title: Active graph metadata will not safely self-resolve from checkbox-only completion
findingDetails.scopeProof: graph-metadata.json:164 carries the retired entity and tasks.md:72 still contains the retired label as source text, so checkbox-only completion plus metadata regeneration is not proven to clear the entity.

p1_001_anchor_fix.notes: Rename both TOC and heading to Spec Kit Framework and update the TOC target to #spec-kit-framework; no additional live anchor-dependent consumer was found beyond README/spec/review evidence.

p1_002_goal_promotion.notes: The plan must include a TOC entry and preserve every current Utility fact in the new FEATURES subsection before trimming the Utility item to a cross-reference.

p1_003_metadata_self_resolution.notes: Regeneration-after-checkbox-only is insufficiently specified because source task text still contains the retired label.

p1_004_deeploop_safety_sentence.notes: A bare runtime README link is insufficient; root README needs a sentence naming permission/sandbox boundary plus stall watchdog, per-lineage cost cap, and lag-ceiling/overrun guardrails.

checklist_evidence.notes: Final checklist should require proof that metadata no longer contains the retired entity.

summary: README TOC and heading rename to Spec Kit Framework is sufficient if the TOC anchor becomes #spec-kit-framework; no other live anchor consumer found.
Goal Plugin promotion must include TOC entry and preserve current Utility facts before trimming the Utility item to a cross-reference.
Metadata regeneration after checkbox-only completion is not safely self-resolving because the source task text still contains the retired label.
Root README needs a specific safety-posture sentence; a bare link to runtime docs is not enough.
